title: Sorted function call should not be passed to the reversed function as an argument
description: Avoid passing the result of the sorted function to the reversed function as an argument. This can lead to unexpected results and performance issues.
recommendation: Refactor your code to avoid using sorted(...) with reversed(...).

noncompliant.description: Example of using sorted with reversed.

compliant.description: Example of using slicing to reverse the sorted list.

why_is_this_an_issue: The sorted function returns a new list, and applying reversed to that list creates a new list again, resulting in two list creations and copying. This can lead to performance issues and unnecessary memory usage.
how_to_fix: Refactor your code to avoid using the reversed function with the sorted function's result. Use slicing or other methods to reverse the list.
function_code:
def check_sorted_with_reversed(node):
    return node.value.args and node.value.args[0].value.name == 'reversed' and 'sorted' in node.ancestors(include_self=True)

metadata.title: Sorted function call should not be passed to the reversed function as an argument
